title: Wash Your Hands
summary: Wash your hands the right way to lower the chance of getting sick or spreading germs to others.
learnerPromise: Wash your hands properly to avoid getting sick or spreading germs.
whyItMatters: Germs collect on your hands throughout the day from surfaces, food, and contact with others. Proper handwashing is one of the simplest ways to stay healthy.
realLifeUses: Washing hands before eating or preparing food
Cleaning hands after using the restroom
Washing up after coming home from public places
Preventing the spread of germs to family or friends
youWillLearn: When to wash your hands during the day
How long to scrub for effective cleaning
Which areas of the hands people often miss
How to dry hands after washing
miniChallenge: Wash your hands once, timing yourself for at least 20 seconds.
steps: Wet your hands with clean running water.
Apply soap and rub your palms together to create a lather.
Scrub the backs of your hands, between your fingers, and under your nails for at least 20 seconds.
Rinse your hands well under clean running water.
Dry your hands with a clean towel or air dryer.
completionCriteria: You wet, lathered, scrubbed, rinsed, and dried your hands
You scrubbed for at least 20 seconds
You cleaned between fingers and under nails
commonProblems: You are not sure you scrubbed long enough — hum a short song twice to time it
No soap is available — use hand sanitizer with at least 60 percent alcohol as a backup
Your hands get dry from frequent washing — apply lotion after drying
tips: Sing the happy birthday song twice to reach about 20 seconds
Scrub under rings and nails where germs can hide
Wash hands before eating, after using the restroom, and after touching shared surfaces
helpBoundary: Ask a medical professional if you have a skin condition that makes hand-washing painful.
